name: Professor Graeme Earl
title: Dean of College of Humanities, Professor of Archaeology
department: School of Arts & Department of Music
expertise_topics: Graeme's research focuses on the intersection of cultural heritage and digital technologies
and in particular how this enables documentation

analysis and sharing of archaeology. He has worked with museums and cultural heritage organisations worldwide to support access to cultural collections in ways that make the best both of physical and digital encounters. His current research is examining interaction design in museums
and in particular the possibilities of mixed reality and the internet of things
research_interests: Graeme's research focuses on the intersection of cultural heritage and digital technologies, and in particular how this enables documentation, analysis and sharing of archaeology. He has worked with museums and cultural heritage organisations worldwide to support access to cultural collections in ways that make the best both of physical and digital encounters. His current research is examining interaction design in museums, and in particular the possibilities of mixed reality and the internet of things.
